lede: A look at December 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jean Racine and Giacomo Puccini, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Don't Ask, Don't Tell Repeal Act of 2010
events.desc: The repeal of the Don't ask, don't tell policy, the 17-year-old policy banning homosexuals serving openly in the United States military, is signed into law by President Barack Obama.

events.title: Kingston Fossil Plant coal fly ash slurry spill
events.desc: An ash dike ruptures at a solid waste containment area for a Tennessee Valley Authority coal-fired power plant in Roane County, Tennessee, releasing 4.2 million m3 (1.1 billion US gal) of coal fly ash slurry in the largest industrial spill in U.S. history.

events.title: Burhanuddin Rabbani
events.desc: Burhanuddin Rabbani, political leader of the Northern Alliance, hands over power in Islamic State of Afghanistan to the interim government headed by President Hamid Karzai.

events.title: Richard Reid
events.desc: Richard Reid attempts to destroy a passenger airliner by igniting explosives hidden in his shoes aboard American Airlines Flight 63.

events.title: Sjafruddin Prawiranegara
events.desc: Sjafruddin Prawiranegara established the Emergency Government of the Republic of Indonesia (Pemerintah Darurat Republik Indonesia, PDRI) in West Sumatra.

events.title: Adolf Hitler
events.desc: World War II: Adolf Hitler signs the order to develop the V-2 rocket as a weapon.

events.title: Lincoln Tunnel
events.desc: The Lincoln Tunnel opens to traffic in New York City.

events.title: Ludwig van Beethoven
events.desc: Ludwig van Beethoven conducts and performs in concert at the Theater an der Wien, Vienna, with the premiere of his Fifth Symphony, Sixth Symphony, Fourth Piano Concerto and Choral Fantasy.

events.title: 856 Damghan earthquake
events.desc: Damghan earthquake: An earthquake near the Persian city of Damghan kills an estimated 200,000 people, the sixth deadliest earthquake in recorded history.

events.title: Pope Innocent I
events.desc: Pope Innocent I is elected, the only pope to succeed his father in the office.

deaths.text: Samuel Beckett, Irish author, poet, and playwright, Nobel Prize laureate
Paul de Lagarde, German biblical scholar and orientalist

observances.text: Christian feast day: Anastasia of Sirmium (Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Ernan, Son of Eogan
Christian feast day: Frances Xavier Cabrini (outside US)
